title: Inkcubeko celebrates cultural heritage
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Inkcubeko Youth and...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Inkcubeko Youth and Science Centre held an arts festival on Heritage Day, 24 September, at Tyholorha Primary School in Thembalethu.

The vibrant event showcased artistic talent and a profound tribute to rich cultural heritage.


> It was attended by artists and performers from schools and NGOs in Thembalethu, as well as seasoned choirs from across the Southern Cape.


The event was a celebration of the diverse traditions that shape eveybody's identity through visual arts, music, and traditional dance.

The group from Siyakhona Youth Development. Photo: Jacques du Toit

Tada Modelling’s models entertained the crowd with their creative expression and runway walks. Photo: Jacques du Toit

The Thembalethu Primary School folklore group showcased their cultural heritage.

‘Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’
